pr.title: fix(ui): add dark mode support to settings pages

comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The class strings for both the active and inactive section buttons are extremely cluttered with duplicated and conflicting dark mode classes. This makes the code very difficult to read and maintain, and can lead to unpredictable rendering.

For example, the active button has multiple conflicting `dark:bg-*` and `dark:text-*` classes.

This issue of duplicated and conflicting classes appears to be systemic throughout the PR. Please perform a thorough cleanup across all modified files to ensure the Tailwind classes are clean, correct, and maintainable.

```suggestion
                    ? 'bg-slate-900 text-white dark:bg-slate-100 dark:text-slate-900'
                    : 'border border-slate-300 bg-white text-slate-700 hover:border-slate-400 hover:text-slate-900 dark:bg-transparent dark:border-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300 dark:hover:border-slate-500 dark:hover:text-white'
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This `className` contains several duplicated and conflicting Tailwind classes for dark mode. This makes the code harder to maintain and can lead to unpredictable styling.

- `dark:border-slate-800` is duplicated.
- `dark:bg-slate-900` is duplicated.
- `dark:bg-slate-100` is duplicated and conflicts with `dark:bg-slate-900`.

This pattern of messy classes appears throughout the PR. Please review all changed files for similar issues.

```suggestion
    <div className="rounded-3xl border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-800 bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 p-6 shadow-sm">
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg) There are conflicting dark mode text color classes: `dark:text-white` and `dark:text-slate-900`. This can cause inconsistent styling. Please remove the redundant class. ```suggestion

Managed Secrets

```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This `className` contains duplicated Tailwind CSS classes for dark mode (`dark:border-amber-900/50`, `dark:bg-amber-900/20`, `dark:text-amber-400`). This is likely a copy-paste error and should be cleaned up.

```suggestion
      <section className="rounded-3xl border border-amber-200 dark:border-amber-900/50 bg-amber-50 dark:bg-amber-900/20 p-6 text-sm text-amber-900 dark:text-amber-400 shadow-sm">
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The class strings for both the error and success notices contain duplicated Tailwind classes for dark mode. This should be cleaned up to improve readability and prevent potential styling conflicts.

```suggestion
                ? 'border-rose-200 dark:border-rose-900/50 bg-rose-50 dark:bg-rose-900/20 text-rose-700 dark:text-rose-400'
                : 'border-emerald-200 dark:border-emerald-900/50 bg-emerald-50 dark:bg-emerald-900/20 text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400'
```

## Code Review

This pull request implements dark mode support across the settings and secret management dashboard by adding Tailwind CSS dark utility classes. The changes affect the Secret Manager, Operations Settings, and Provider Profiles components. Feedback focuses on cleaning up the Tailwind class strings, as many elements contain duplicated or conflicting dark mode classes that could lead to inconsistent styling and maintenance difficulties.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This `className` has an excessive number of conflicting dark mode background classes (`dark:bg-slate-900`, `dark:bg-slate-100`, `dark:bg-slate-800`, `dark:bg-slate-200/50`). Please simplify this to a single intended style for dark mode to ensure correctness and improve maintainability.

```suggestion
                className="rounded-full border border-sky-200 bg-white dark:border-sky-800 dark:bg-slate-800 dark:text-slate-300 px-2.5 py-1 text-xs"
```

The container/heading uses conflicting `dark:bg-*` and `dark:text-*` classes (`dark:bg-slate-900` and `dark:bg-slate-100`, `dark:text-white` and `dark:text-slate-900`). In addition, this component still uses hard-coded light-theme inline colors in the table (e.g. `#666`, `#ddd`), which will have poor contrast once the container background becomes dark. Please dedupe the dark classes and migrate the table styling to Tailwind classes with appropriate `dark:` variants.

The header container includes conflicting/duplicated Tailwind dark-mode classes (e.g., both `dark:bg-slate-900` and `dark:bg-slate-100`, and `dark:border-slate-800` repeated). Tailwind will apply the *last* matching class, which can negate the intended dark theme and makes styling brittle—please dedupe and choose a single dark background/border value.

The section tab button class strings contain multiple conflicting dark-mode classes (`dark:bg-*` and `dark:text-*` appear more than once with different values). This results in unpredictable visuals (last class wins) and makes future edits error-prone—please remove the duplicates and keep a single intended dark variant for background/text/hover states.

The reset/create button includes contradictory `dark:text-*` classes (both `dark:text-slate-300` and `dark:text-slate-900`/`dark:text-white` appear). Because Tailwind resolves by order, the final text color may not match the intended design; please remove duplicates and set one consistent dark text color.
```suggestion
          className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full border border-slate-300 dark:border-slate-700 px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-300 transition hover:border-slate-400 dark:hover:border-slate-500 hover:text-slate-900 dark:hover:text-white"
```

The managed secret slug <code> badge has multiple conflicting dark background classes (`dark:bg-slate-900`, `dark:bg-slate-100`, `dark:bg-slate-800`, `dark:bg-slate-200/50`). Only one will take effect, so the actual color is order-dependent; please simplify to a single intended dark background.
```suggestion
                className="rounded-full border border-sky-200 bg-white px-2.5 py-1 text-xs dark:border-sky-800 dark:bg-slate-800 dark:text-slate-300"
```

The pause submit button includes both `dark:bg-slate-100` and `dark:bg-slate-200` (and duplicates), which means the rendered dark-mode background is order-dependent. Please keep only one dark background value and one dark hover value to avoid unintended overrides.

The resume button class list repeats `dark:bg-emerald-500` and also sets `dark:text-slate-900`, which may reduce contrast on an emerald background. Please dedupe the repeated dark background class and ensure the dark-mode text color has sufficient contrast for accessibility.
```suggestion
                      className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-emerald-600 dark:bg-emerald-500 px-5 py-2.5 text-sm font-semibold text-white dark:text-white transition hover:bg-emerald-500 dark:hover:bg-emerald-400"
```

This section wrapper has conflicting dark-mode background classes (`dark:bg-slate-900` and `dark:bg-slate-100` are both present, and repeated). In dark mode the later class will override earlier ones, potentially producing a light background while other text uses dark-mode colors; please dedupe and keep a single `dark:bg-*` value.
```suggestion
    <section className="rounded-3xl border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-800 bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 p-6 shadow-sm">
```

This section wrapper repeats/conflicts dark-mode classes (`dark:bg-slate-900` and `dark:bg-slate-100` both present, with duplicates). This can override the intended dark background and makes the resulting styling dependent on class order; please dedupe and choose a single dark background/border set.

## Pull request overview Adds dark mode styling to the Settings page and its related components so they follow the app’s dark theme, and updates the built frontend assets accordingly. **Changes:** - Added Tailwind `dark:` variants for backgrounds, borders, and text across Settings UI surfaces. - Updated Settings subcomponents (Operations, Provider Profiles, Secrets) to include dark-mode styling. - Rebuilt static frontend bundle and updated Vite manifest to reference the new settings asset. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | frontend/src/entrypoints/settings.tsx | Adds dark-mode styles to the main Settings page layout, section tabs, and notices. | | frontend/src/components/settings/ProviderProfilesManager.tsx | Adds dark-mode styles to provider profiles table, buttons, and form controls. | | frontend/src/components/settings/OperationsSettingsSection.tsx | Adds dark-mode styles to operational controls, status panels, and notices. | | frontend/src/components/secrets/SecretManager.tsx | Adds dark-mode styles to managed secrets container/headers and section borders. | | api_service/static/task_dashboard/dist/assets/settings-BWye1uVn.js | Rebuilt/minified settings bundle reflecting source changes. | | api_service/static/task_dashboard/dist/assets/settings-B8eL4aY2.js | Removes the previous settings bundle artifact. | | api_service/static/task_dashboard/dist/.vite/manifest.json | Updates manifest to point settings entry to the new bundle filename. |
